content:
The tells that kill your traffic are not the ones a detector can count. They are the ones your reader feels and cannot name.

14 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A person scrolling a phone with a blank, disengaged expression.I built a machine that reads prose and scores how much of it looks written by AI.
Then I tuned it against ten real articles by Nicolas Cole, one of the most read writers on the internet.
You believe AI writing is basically indistinguishable now.
So you generate a draft, you tidy it, you hit publish, and you wait for the reads.
They do not come.
Then I pointed the same detector at synthetic prose. It failed. Hard.
The machine could count the obvious signatures all day. It could not find the thing that actually makes a reader leave.
Because the reader was never running my checklist.
The reader was feeling something, and then closing the tab.
What you think is going wrong
When the reads dry up, writers reach for the usual suspects.

• The headline was weak

• The algorithm changed again
